hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric discrimination against SSTR3/5 by exploiting SSTR2-specific subpockets. These changes will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack sufficient selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to break conserved off-target interactions and increase Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 that are likely driving off-target binding. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated to reduce off-target affinity.
Introducing mutations at these positions to break conserved hydrogen bonds or hydrophobic interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while avoiding disruption of SSTR2 binding.
